title: [Mac] Unable to push to registry but able to login to it
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

For a openshift cluster we can use the port-forward to expose a service on localhost and trying to expose the openshift internal registry service on host on port 5000. Now when login to this registry using podman works as expected but not able to push the image.

```
$ oc -n openshift-image-registry port-forward service/image-registry 5000:5000
Forwarding from 127.0.0.1:5000 -> 5000
Forwarding from [::1]:5000 -> 5000
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Have an local registry which is not run by podman only expose to the host. I used the openshift internal registry with port forwarding.

```
$ podman login --tls-verify=false -u kubeadmin -p $(oc whoami -t) 127.0.0.1:5000 --log-level debug     
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug          
DEBU[0000] Called login.PersistentPreRunE(podman login --tls-verify=false -u kubeadmin -p sha256~crXqzvc8kCYtXze_1GO1VSBwzzkmLj27qzugfLYROzQ 127.0.0.1:5000 --log-level debug) 
DEBU[0000] SSH Ident Key "/Users/prkumar/.ssh/podman-machine-default" SHA256:d8g6aDUw0r74mIenChTniBJ7oyZA+QyehCWJw2GsaWk ssh-ed25519 
DEBU[0000] Found SSH_AUTH_SOCK "/private/tmp/com.apple.launchd.M9JsC4HPcx/Listeners", ssh-agent signer(s) enabled 
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v4.1.0/libpod/_ping 
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf" 
DEBU[0000] No credentials matching 127.0.0.1:5000 found in /Users/prkumar/.config/containers/auth.json 
DEBU[0000] No credentials matching 127.0.0.1:5000 found in /Users/prkumar/.config/containers/auth.json 
DEBU[0000] No credentials matching 127.0.0.1:5000 found in /Users/prkumar/.docker/config.json 
DEBU[0000] No credentials matching 127.0.0.1:5000 found in /Users/prkumar/.dockercfg 
DEBU[0000] No credentials for 127.0.0.1:5000 found      
DEBU[0000] Looking for TLS certificates and private keys in /etc/docker/certs.d/127.0.0.1:5000 
DEBU[0000] GET https://127.0.0.1:5000/v2/               
DEBU[0001] Ping https://127.0.0.1:5000/v2/ status 401   
DEBU[0001] GET https://127.0.0.1:5000/openshift/token?account=kubeadmin 
DEBU[0002] Increasing token expiration to: 60 seconds   
DEBU[0002] GET https://127.0.0.1:5000/v2/               
DEBU[0003] Stored credentials for 127.0.0.1:5000 in credential helper containers-auth.json 
Login Succeeded!
DEBU[0003] Called login.PersistentPostRunE(podman login --tls-verify=false -u kubeadmin -p sha256~crXqzvc8kCYtXze_1GO1VSBwzzkmLj27qzugfLYROzQ 127.0.0.1:5000 --log-level debug)


✗ podman pull busybox
Resolved "busybox" as an alias (/etc/containers/registries.conf.d/000-shortnames.conf)
Trying to pull docker.io/library/busybox:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:50e8d59317eb665383b2ef4d9434aeaa394dcd6f54b96bb7810fdde583e9c2d1

Copying config sha256:1a80408de790c0b1075d0a7e23ff7da78b311f85f36ea10098e4a6184c200964

Storing signatures
1a80408de790c0b1075d0a7e23ff7da78b311f85f36ea10098e4a6184c200964

✗ podman tag busybox 127.0.0.1:5000/openshift/busybox

✗ podman push --tls-verify=false 127.0.0.1:5000/openshift/busybox --log-level debug
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug          
DEBU[0000] Called push.PersistentPreRunE(podman push 127.0.0.1:5000/openshift/busybox --log-level debug) 
DEBU[0000] SSH Ident Key "/Users/prkumar/.ssh/podman-machine-default" SHA256:d8g6aDUw0r74mIenChTniBJ7oyZA+QyehCWJw2GsaWk ssh-ed25519 
DEBU[0000] Found SSH_AUTH_SOCK "/private/tmp/com.apple.launchd.M9JsC4HPcx/Listeners", ssh-agent signer(s) enabled 
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v4.1.0/libpod/_ping 
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf" 
DEBU[0000] Found credentials for 127.0.0.1:5000 in credential helper containers-auth.json in file /Users/prkumar/.config/containers/auth.json 
DEBU[0000] Found credentials for 127.0.0.1:8888 in credential helper containers-auth.json in file /Users/prkumar/.config/containers/auth.json 
DEBU[0000] Found credentials for default-route-openshift-image-registry.apps-crc.testing in credential helper containers-auth.json in file /Users/prkumar/.config/containers/auth.json 
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: POST URI: http://d/v4.1.0/libpod/images/127.0.0.1:5000/openshift/busybox/push 
Error: error pushing image "127.0.0.1:5000/openshift/busybox": trying to reuse blob sha256:eb6b01329ebe73e209e44a616a0e16c2b8e91de6f719df9c35e6cdadadbe5965 at destination: pinging container registry 127.0.0.1:5000: Get "https://127.0.0.1:5000/v2/": dial tcp 127.0.0.1:5000: connect: connection refused
```

**Describe the results you received:**
Not able to push the image to registry

**Describe the results you expected:**
Should able to push the image to registry

**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
✗ podman version
Client:       Podman Engine
Version:      4.1.0

Built:        Fri Apr  1 23:51:54 2022
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64

```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


**Additional details**

As per push logs I think since the podman command actually going to execute in the VM and since VM doesn't have info about that `127.0.0.1:5000` port so it is failing (check the POST request) It is bit strange though that login works (doesn't need any POST request). Is there a way to expose port from the host to VM  (using like gvproxy) ?

comments.body:
~~@praveenkumar Thanks for creating the issue. Have you tried `podman push --tls-verify=false 127.0.0.1:5000/openshift/busybox` ?~~

Edit: nvm i misread for linux this is podman-remote on macOS.

use `host.containers.internal` (this will resolve to an IP which allows you to access the actual host loopback) instead of 127.0.0.1.
I don't think this is a podman issue. Login works because login is always on the client side.
@Luap99 I will try that and let you know.

So it is like chicken and egg problem, host doesn't aware about `host.containers.internal` but VM does so I had to do something following to make it work.
 - First try to login with `podman login --tls-verify=false -u kubeadmin -p $(oc whoami -t) 127.0.0.1:5000`
 - Then edit the `vi ~/.config/containers/auth.json` file manually and add the copy entry of `127.0.0.1:5000` registry
```json
{
	"auths": {
		"127.0.0.1:5000": {
			"auth": "a3ViZWFkbWluOnNoYTI1Nn5HeUYyNnNBTHExLVQ0Ny1EcVp4UVp2OTNVWT"
		},
		"host.containers.internal:5000": {
			"auth": "a3ViZWFkbWluOnNoYTI1Nn5HeUYyNnNBTHExLVQ0Ny1EcVp4UVp2OTNVWT"
		}
}
```
- And finally able to push 
```
$ podman tag busybox host.containers.internal:5000/openshift/busybox
$ podman push --tls-verify=false host.containers.internal:5000/openshift/busybox  --log-level debug
INFO[0000] podman filtering at log level debug          
DEBU[0000] Called push.PersistentPreRunE(podman push --tls-verify=false host.containers.internal:5000/openshift/busybox --log-level debug) 
DEBU[0000] SSH Ident Key "/Users/prkumar/.ssh/podman-machine-default" SHA256:d8g6aDUw0r74mIenChTniBJ7oyZA+QyehCWJw2GsaWk ssh-ed25519 
DEBU[0000] Found SSH_AUTH_SOCK "/private/tmp/com.apple.launchd.M9JsC4HPcx/Listeners", ssh-agent signer(s) enabled 
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: GET URI: http://d/v4.1.0/libpod/_ping 
DEBU[0000] Loading registries configuration "/etc/containers/registries.conf" 
DEBU[0000] Found credentials for 127.0.0.1:5000 in credential helper containers-auth.json in file /Users/prkumar/.config/containers/auth.json 
DEBU[0000] Found credentials for host.containers.internal:5000 in credential helper containers-auth.json in file /Users/prkumar/.config/containers/auth.json 
DEBU[0000] Found credentials for default-route-openshift-image-registry.apps-crc.testing in credential helper containers-auth.json in file /Users/prkumar/.config/containers/auth.json 
DEBU[0000] DoRequest Method: POST URI: http://d/v4.1.0/libpod/images/host.containers.internal:5000/openshift/busybox/push 
DEBU[0017] Called push.PersistentPostRunE(podman push --tls-verify=false host.containers.internal:5000/openshift/busybox --log-level debug) 
```

I am closing this issue because I also think it is not something podman fault or can do anything around it.
